query: Cognition Devin Review Security Swarm automated PR scan bug detection rate comparison 2026

{"query":"Cognition Devin Review Security Swarm automated PR scan bug detection rate comparison 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1289,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cognition-launches-devin-security-swarm-to-tackle-the-vulnerability-backlog-302814800.html","title":"Cognition Launches Devin Security Swarm to Tackle the Vulnerability Backlog","content":"Cognition launches Devin Security Swarm to help security teams find, validate, and remediate exploitable vulnerabilities.
Monthly security findings throughout the enterprise are climbing from about 1,000 to more than 10,000 in six months, driven in part by the 42% of code that is now AI-generated or AI-assisted.
For an enterprise security team, the real question is whether a tool performs at scale and proves its findings are worth acting on.
This is more than any other AI-powered scanner tested, at 30% lower cost per finding than the next most accurate alternative.
Devin works end-to-end on complex engineering tasks, from planning and writing code to testing and remediating security vulnerabilities, giving teams the capacity to take on more ambitious work."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/cognition-ai-labs_security-teams-do-not-need-more-untriaged-activity-7478134221413249026-dkAp","title":"Devin Security Swarm Finds Vulnerabilities at 30% Lower Cost | Cognition posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"Join our launch webinar on July 7 at 5PM UK / 6PM CET for a demo and Q&A with the Cognition team.
Devin validates findings from the scanners you already use and ships the fixes as PRs.
Devin Security Swarm finds real vulnerabilities across complex codebases, validates exploitability at runtime in isolated sandboxes, and ships remediation PRs that make it to production.
Validate exploitability at runtime, catch novel vulnerabilities such as chained CVEs and business logic vulns, scan thousands of repos, and have security folks contribute the actual fix."},{"url":"https://devin.ai/blog/security-swarm-eval","title":"Evaluating Security Swarm | Devin","content":"Security Swarm finds the bug in 72% of cases, the highest recall of any tool we tested, at roughly two-thirds the cost of the next most accurate alternative.
We want to know whether Devin reasoned about the code to detect a real vulnerability, not whether a model recognized a CVE it had already seen in training.
We built a dataset of 50 vulnerabilities across 14 languages including Go, Rust, Python, Ruby, Java, C#, JavaScript, C, Swift, Dart, and Elixir.
vulnerability: "RCE via eval() on server-supplied vector data" cwe: "CWE-94"
Note that we require the specific vulnerability, not just any real bug in the right file.
It leads on both detection and economics at once, rather than trading one for the other.
We report here the average cost per scan across the 50 runs."},{"url":"https://cognition.com/blog/introducing-devin-security-swarm","title":"Introducing Devin Security Swarm | Cognition","content":"Security Swarm finds more verified vulnerabilities at 30% lower cost than the nearest comparable alternative.
After Devin confirms a vulnerability, it writes the patch and opens a PR for review.
For the full technical breakdown, read our blog post on the Agentic MapReduce architecture.
First, Devin burns down your CVE backlog."},{"url":"https://www.aol.com/articles/cognition-launches-devin-security-swarm-130000000.html","title":"Cognition Launches Devin Security Swarm to Tackle the Vulnerability Backlog - AOL","content":""Devin Security Swarm gives security teams engineering capacity they've never had," said Nick Wong, Security Engineering Lead at Cognition.
Provable performance at a lower cost: On a benchmark of 50 real-world vulnerabilities tied to published GitHub Security Advisories across 14 languages, Devin Security Swarm found 36.
For more information on the Devin Security Program, visit devin.ai/security-program."},{"url":"https://app.dealroom.co/news/feed/cognition-launches-devin-security-swarm-to-validate-and-fix-exploitable-vulnerabilities-as-ai-code-generation-outpaces-security-teams","title":"Cognition launches Devin Security Swarm to validate and fix ...","content":"Jul 2, 2026 — In benchmarking against 50 real-world vulnerabilities across 14 languages, it identified 36 flaws—more than any competing AI scanner—at 30% ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Cognition Devin Review Security Swarm automated PR scan bug detection rate comparison 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://byteiota.com/devin-security-swarm-ai-catches-72-of-bugs-at-90","title":"Devin Security Swarm: AI Catches 72% of Bugs at $90 | byteiota","content":"Data visualization showing Devin Security Swarm benchmark comparison with four AI security tools, parallel agent nodes scanning code, and security shield icons. AI-generated code now accounts for 42% of what ships to production — and it’s introducing security findings at **10x the rate** of human-written code. Security teams that once reviewed roughly 1,000 findings a month are now staring at 10,000-plus, most of them false positives from tools that can’t reason about business logic. Cognition’s answer is **Devin Security Swarm**, launched this week: parallel AI agents that scan your entire codebase, validate whether each vulnerability is actually exploitable in a live sandbox, and write the remediation PRs themselves — for around $90 a scan. Devin Security Swarm is an AI tool designed to clear it. Security Swarm is a direct extension of that platform — and a telling sign of where AI coding tool security is heading in 2026.","score":0.7927375,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.idlen.io/blog/devin-ai-engineer-review-limits-2026","title":"Devin, the AI Engineer: Review, Testing & Limitations in 2026","content":"3. As AI code production accelerates, security teams are facing a growing pile of findings they can't act on. Security teams are stuck with tools that haven't caught up. AI security tools often struggle to reason effectively about the whole codebase. None of them can validate which findings are actually exploitable or write a fix. Devin Security Swarm brings engineering capabilities to security teams so they can ship fixes themselves. It finds vulnerabilities across the codebase, validates that they are exploitable at runtime, and ships remediation PRs. Security Swarm finds more verified vulnerabilities at 30% lower cost than the nearest comparable alternative. What reaches the security team is a set of confirmed-exploitable vulnerabilities with attack paths and steps to reproduce. The first full scan establishes a baseline across your codebase.
